summary: Gruber alerts Daring Fireball members-only RSS feed subscribers who use Bloglines that they need to manually mark their subscriptions as private. Even after embedding authentication credentials in the feed URL, Bloglines defaults to making all subscriptions public, which would expose members' login credentials. The post provides step-by-step instructions for both new and existing subscribers to fix this privacy issue.
key_points: Bloglines does not default to treating authenticated feeds as private, exposing membership credentials publicly
Users must manually change the Access setting to 'Private' when subscribing to password-protected feeds
The post provides specific instructions for both new subscriptions and updating existing ones
money_quotes: One would think that a web-based aggregator would default to treating feeds which require a username and password as private — but one would be wrong.
key_insight: Bloglines' default of making authenticated feed subscriptions public is a surprising and problematic design choice that exposes users' credentials.
